description: Manifest for the on-demand-fetched real-world scenes used in SplatBench. The PLY bytes themselves are NOT committed (gitignored — hundreds of MB each). Each entry below is reproducible from the listed source URL; the `hash` is computed by `splatforge analyze` and matches the `hash` field on the corresponding row in benches/reports/splatbench-v0.json.

scenes.license: Mip-NeRF 360 dataset (open research; CC-BY-4.0). Original capture and scene © Mip-NeRF 360 authors (Barron et al., CVPR 2022). Re-trained as 3D Gaussian Splats by the Inria GraphDeco group, mirrored on HuggingFace by dylanebert. Used here for reproducible benchmarking under the same terms as the existing bonsai/bicycle entries.
scenes.substitutionRationale: Original target was the canonical 'Sweet Corals' outdoor-coral photogrammetry tile (sc-tile2 in private research, 2.8M splats, used in PostHAC + SwVQ + diff-repack validation). That asset lives on a private Blurry Drive with no public canonical URL — SplatBench is a public corpus and cannot depend on internal credentials. Mip-NeRF 360 garden/flowers were not available as Inria-PLY on the public HuggingFace mirror (only `.splat` files). Mip-NeRF 360 `stump` is the closest available real outdoor 3DGS scene with a public Inria-PLY mirror, complements `bicycle` (organic foliage + tree-stump texture vs bicycle-on-grass), and gives the leaderboard a second large real-outdoor anchor.

scenes.rationale: Indoor close-up macro photogrammetry (cluster fly / Pollenia). Adds the missing indoor close-up texture profile to a corpus currently dominated by Mip-NeRF 360 outdoor scenes. 5-LOD ladder (S→XXL: 25k → 3.5M splats) lets SplatBench measure how ratios and fidelity scale with splat count on the same scene.
